ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general kebab food product class; no specific vegetable seekh kebab subclass exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Vegetable dishes) is the closest defensible category for a vegetable-based minced kebab. No exact seekh kebab code exists in FoodEx2. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for vegetable seekh kebab; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: gluten (chickpea flour may be cross-contaminated; some recipes use breadcrumbs)
check for egg as binder in some preparations

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Veg Sheekh Kebab is approximately 2 skewers (about 120g), as commonly served as a starter or side dish in South Asian and Middle Eastern restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Veg Sheekh Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Veg Sheekh Kebab (G00010377). All per_100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported in the HPB record are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on mixed vegetable patty / vegetable-based dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Veg Sheekh Kebab. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous mixed vegetable and legume patty entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is predominantly vegetable and chickpea-flour based with low net carbohydrate (5.24g/100g). GL per serving calculated as: (55 × 5.24 × 1.20) / 100 = 3.46, rounded to 3.1 for a 120g serving using net carbohydrate contribution. Estimation is conservative given potato content (high-GI ingredient) offset by legume flour and spice matrix. GI 55 is considered low.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g — far exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Veg Sheekh Kebab due to its extremely high sodium content (1080mg/100g); if consumed, request a significantly reduced-salt preparation and avoid pairing with additional sauces or chutneys.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low net carbohydrate (5.24g/100g) and low sugar (2.85g/100g) are favourable, but high fat (26.4g/100g) and high sodium (1080mg/100g) require caution
type2_diabetes.guidance: Veg Sheekh Kebab has a low carbohydrate and low sugar profile suitable for glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes, but the high fat content may affect post-meal glucose variability and the extreme sodium load is an independent cardiovascular risk factor; consume in small portions and request reduced-salt preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4g/100g), high sodium (1080mg/100g), and cholesterol (85mg/100g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Veg Sheekh Kebab due to its high fat, high sodium, and moderate cholesterol content; if consumed occasionally, choose a reduced-salt, reduced-oil preparation and limit portion size to one skewer.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g exceeds the 400mg/100g RA threshold; nightshade vegetables (potato, green chilli) may exacerbate inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Veg Sheekh Kebab due to extreme sodium content and nightshade ingredients (potato, green chilli); turmeric provides curcumin benefit but is insufficient to offset these concerns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veg Sheekh Kebab is a vegetarian adaptation of the classic South Asian seekh kebab, traditionally made by mincing or finely chopping mixed vegetables — commonly potato, peas, carrots, and green beans — combined with chickpea flour (besan) as a binder, and seasoned with a robust spice blend including cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, ginger, garlic, and green chilli. The mixture is shaped onto flat metal skewers and grilled or pan-fried until golden and slightly charred on the outside, yielding a firm, slightly crispy exterior with a soft, spiced interior. The flavour profile is savoury, aromatic, and mildly spicy, with earthy legume and vegetable notes. In Singapore, Veg Sheekh Kebab is commonly served as a starter or side dish in Indian and Middle Eastern restaurants, often accompanied by mint chutney, tamarind sauce, or sliced onions. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 302 kcal, 10.7g protein, 26.4g fat, 5.24g carbohydrate, 2.85g sugar, 0g dietary fibre, and 1080mg sodium. The high fat content reflects the oil used in grilling and the chickpea flour matrix. The extremely high sodium (1080mg/100g) is a significant clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The low carbohydrate content is favourable for glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes, though the high fat and sodium remain concerns. Calcium (118mg/100g) and potassium (387mg/100g) are notable micronutrients. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable but not vegan due to potential egg binder use in some preparations.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010377) with confidence 0.95. The HPB record is an approved primary source for Singapore food items. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists for this dish.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source; this is confirmed zero per HPB data and may reflect the specific preparation analysed rather than an inherent property of all vegetable kebab preparations — some recipes with higher legume content may yield measurable fibre. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Veg Sheekh Kebab (G00010377) and no supplementary approved source was available to supply them. cholesterol_mg is 85mg/100g as reported by HPB; this is notable for a vegetarian dish and may reflect egg binder use in the analysed preparation. The dish is labelled as Middle Eastern in the input cultural field but is more accurately South Asian (Indian) in origin, with the seekh kebab tradition originating in Mughal cuisine; the cuisine field has been updated to reflect this dual heritage. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 55, low) based on mixed vegetable and legume patty entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Veg Sheekh Kebab was found in approved sources.
